grammar_points.title: 不仅...而且... — Not only...but also...
grammar_points.explanation: 不仅...而且... is used to emphasize that something has more than one quality or characteristic. 不仅 introduces the first point and 而且 introduces the additional, often stronger or more surprising point. Both clauses share the same subject. Example: 他不仅聪明，而且很勤奋。(He is not only smart, but also very hardworking.)

examples.chinese: 她不仅漂亮，而且非常聪明。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù jǐn piào liàng, ér qiě fēi cháng cōng míng.
examples.english: She is not only beautiful, but also very smart.

examples.chinese: 这个地方不仅安静，而且风景很美。
examples.pinyin: Zhè ge dì fāng bù jǐn ān jìng, ér qiě fēng jǐng hěn měi.
examples.english: This place is not only quiet, but also has beautiful scenery.

exercises.question: Choose the correct sentence using 不仅...而且...
exercises.correct: 他不仅幽默，而且非常体贴。
options: 他不仅幽默，而且非常体贴。
他不仅幽默，所以非常体贴。
option_pinyin: Tā bù jǐn yōu mò, ér qiě fēi cháng tǐ tiē.
Tā bù jǐn yōu mò, suǒ yǐ fēi cháng tǐ tiē.
Tā yōu mò, bù jǐn ér qiě tǐ tiē.
Tā yōu mò bù jǐn, ér qiě tǐ tiē.

grammar_points.title: 为了 — In order to / For the sake of
grammar_points.explanation: 为了 expresses purpose or motivation. It means 'in order to' or 'for the sake of' and is placed at the beginning of the sentence or before the verb phrase. The subject can come before or after 为了. Structure: 为了 + goal + subject + verb. Example: 为了健康，他每天锻炼。(In order to stay healthy, he exercises every day.)

examples.chinese: 为了表示感谢，她送给他一份礼物。
examples.pinyin: Wèi le biǎo shì gǎn xiè, tā sòng gěi tā yí fèn lǐ wù.
examples.english: In order to express gratitude, she gave him a gift.

examples.chinese: 为了让她幸福，他愿意做任何事情。
examples.pinyin: Wèi le ràng tā xìng fú, tā yuàn yì zuò rèn hé shì qíng.
examples.english: In order to make her happy, he is willing to do anything.

exercises.correct: 为了感动她，他唱了一首歌。
options: 为了感动她，他唱了一首歌。
他感动她，为了唱了一首歌。
他唱了，为了一首歌感动她。
为了他，感动她唱了一首歌。
option_pinyin: Wèi le gǎn dòng tā, tā chàng le yì shǒu gē.
Tā gǎn dòng tā, wèi le chàng le yì shǒu gē.
Tā chàng le, wèi le yì shǒu gē gǎn dòng tā.
Wèi le tā, gǎn dòng tā chàng le yì shǒu gē.

grammar_points.title: 却 — But / However (indicating contrast)
grammar_points.explanation: 却 is an adverb placed directly before a verb or adjective to indicate a contrast or an unexpected result. It is similar to 但是 but 却 appears inside the second clause rather than at the beginning. Structure: Subject + 却 + verb/adjective. It often implies surprise or a result that goes against expectations. Example: 他很忙，却每天给她发短信。(He is very busy, but he still messages her every day.)

examples.chinese: 她感觉很幸福，却没有表示出来。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎn jué hěn xìng fú, què méi yǒu biǎo shì chū lái.
examples.english: She felt very happy, but didn't show it.

examples.chinese: 他邀请她吃晚饭，她却说没有时间。
examples.pinyin: Tā yāo qǐng tā chī wǎn fàn, tā què shuō méi yǒu shí jiān.
examples.english: He invited her for dinner, but she said she had no time.

Tā gǎn dòng bù yǐ, méi yǒu kū què.
Tā què, gǎn dòng bù yǐ méi yǒu kū.

culture_notes.title: Chinese Valentine's Day — Qixi Festival
culture_notes.content: The Qixi Festival (七夕节, Qī Xī Jié) falls on the 7th day of the 7th lunar month and is often called Chinese Valentine's Day. It is based on the legend of the Cowherd (牛郎, Niú Láng) and the Weaver Girl (织女, Zhī Nǚ), two star-crossed lovers separated by the Milky Way. According to the legend, they are only allowed to meet once a year when a flock of magpies forms a bridge across the sky. This festival celebrates romantic love and devotion. In modern China, couples exchange gifts, go on dates, and celebrate their relationships on this day, blending ancient tradition with contemporary romance.

idioms.pinyin: yǒu qíng rén zhōng chéng juàn shǔ
idioms.english: Lovers will eventually get together (said of people who are meant to be)

Fill in: 她感觉非常幸福，因为他准备了一个 _____ 的约会。

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 爱玲，我想邀请你这个周末去看电影，你有时间吗？
lines.pinyin: Ài Líng, wǒ xiǎng yāo qǐng nǐ zhè ge zhōu mò qù kàn diàn yǐng, nǐ yǒu shí jiān ma?
lines.english: Eileen, I'd like to invite you to see a movie this weekend. Are you free?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 当然！我感觉这会是一次很浪漫的约会。
lines.pinyin: Dāng rán! Wǒ gǎn jué zhè huì shì yí cì hěn làng màn de yuē huì.
lines.english: Of course! I feel this will be a very romantic date.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我不仅订好了电影票，而且还订了一家餐厅。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ bù jǐn dìng hǎo le diàn yǐng piào, ér qiě hái dìng le yì jiā cān tīng.
lines.english: I not only booked movie tickets, but also reserved a restaurant.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你真的好幽默又体贴！我很感动。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ zhēn de hǎo yōu mò yòu tǐ tiē! Wǒ hěn gǎn dòng.
lines.english: You are really humorous and considerate! I am so touched.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 为了让你幸福，我愿意做任何事。
lines.pinyin: Wèi le ràng nǐ xìng fú, wǒ yuàn yì zuò rèn hé shì.
lines.english: In order to make you happy, I am willing to do anything.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我们的感情虽然简单，却非常真实，我很珍惜。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ men de gǎn qíng suī rán jiǎn dān, què fēi cháng zhēn shí, wǒ hěn zhēn xī.
lines.english: Our relationship is simple but very real. I treasure it greatly.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我也是。我总是想紧紧地抱着你。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ yě shì. Wǒ zǒng shì xiǎng jǐn jǐn de bào zhe nǐ.
lines.english: Me too. I want to hold you tight all the time.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 谢谢你让我感觉这么幸福。
lines.pinyin: Xiè xie nǐ ràng wǒ gǎn jué zhè me xìng fú.
lines.english: Thank you for making me feel so happy.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 但丁，你为什么总是那么骄傲？有时候让人很难接近你。
lines.pinyin: Lán tè, nǐ wèi shén me zǒng shì nà me jiāo ào? Yǒu shí hou ràng rén hěn nán jiē jìn nǐ.
lines.english: Dante, why are you always so arrogant? Sometimes it makes it hard to get close to you.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我知道。我的感情很深，却不善于表示出来。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zhī dào. Wǒ de gǎn qíng hěn shēn, què bú shàn yú biǎo shì chū lái.
lines.english: I know. My feelings run deep, but I am not good at expressing them.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 其实你不仅聪明，而且有很多优点，你应该多表示出来。
lines.pinyin: Qí shí nǐ bù jǐn cōng míng, ér qiě yǒu hěn duō yōu diǎn, nǐ yīng gāi duō biǎo shì chū lái.
lines.english: Actually you are not only smart, but also have many good qualities. You should show them more.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 谢谢你，我会努力变得更幽默。
lines.pinyin: Xiè xie nǐ， wǒ huì nǔ lì biàn de gèng yōu mò.
lines.english: Thank you. I will work hard to become more humorous.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我感觉你已经在改变了，这让我很感动。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ gǎn jué nǐ yǐ jīng zài gǎi biàn le, zhè ràng wǒ hěn gǎn dòng.
lines.english: I feel you are already changing. This really moves me.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 有你这样的朋友，我感觉很幸福。
lines.pinyin: Yǒu nǐ zhè yàng de péng yǒu, wǒ gǎn jué hěn xìng fú.
lines.english: Having a friend like you makes me feel very happy.
